sourceTitle: Get the most out of your Business trial with Notion AI

questions.question: How can you open Notion AI’s Personal Agent for the first time?

options.text: Click the circular face icon in the bottom‑right corner of your workspace
options.explanation: The article says the Agent is accessed by clicking the circular face icon or via the Notion AI tab in the left sidebar.

options.text: Press Ctrl + Shift + A on your keyboard
options.explanation: There is no keyboard shortcut mentioned for opening the Agent.

options.text: Navigate to Settings → Personal Agent
options.explanation: Personal Agent is opened via the face icon or sidebar, not through Settings.

options.text: Type "/agent" on any page
options.explanation: The article does not describe a "/agent" command; the correct method is the face icon.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a capability of the Personal Agent as described in the guide?

options.text: Building databases based on a natural‑language description
options.explanation: The Agent can create databases from a description, as shown in the example.

options.text: Researching the web and creating pages with external information
options.explanation: The guide mentions the Agent can turn on web search to research and create pages.

options.text: Running code snippets directly inside Notion pages
options.explanation: Running code is not listed as a function of the Personal Agent; it focuses on content creation, search, and workflow automation.

options.text: Updating existing pages with new data from connected tools
options.explanation: The Agent can update pages using data from connected tools like Google Drive or Slack.

questions.question: What must you do after selecting a tool (e.g., Google Drive) to connect it with Notion AI?

options.text: Approve the requested permissions and wait for the initial indexing to finish
options.explanation: The guide instructs you to approve permissions and wait for indexing after connecting a tool.

options.text: Manually upload every file from the tool into Notion
options.explanation: Files are indexed automatically; manual upload is not required.

options.text: Create a new workspace for each connected tool
options.explanation: Connecting tools does not require separate workspaces.

options.text: Restart Notion to activate the connector
options.explanation: A restart is not mentioned; only permission approval and indexing are required.

questions.question: When using AI Meeting Notes, which setting enables automatic detection of a Zoom or Google Meet call?

options.text: Settings → Notifications → Meeting detection notifications
options.explanation: The article specifies toggling on Meeting detection notifications in Settings → Notifications.

options.text: Settings → Notion AI → Auto‑record meetings
options.explanation: There is no ‘Auto‑record meetings’ toggle described.

options.text: Workspace → Integrations → Zoom auto‑join
options.explanation: Zoom auto‑join is unrelated to AI Meeting Notes detection.

options.text: User profile → Preferences → Enable meeting transcripts
options.explanation: The setting is located under Settings → Notifications, not user profile preferences.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about AI connectors is accurate?

options.text: After connecting a tool, the Agent can reference its content when building databases or answering questions
options.explanation: The guide explains that once a tool is connected, the Agent can use its content for various tasks.

options.text: Connecting a tool automatically syncs all its data in real‑time with Notion databases
options.explanation: Data is indexed and searchable, but real‑time sync is not guaranteed; one‑way syncs are a separate feature.

options.text: You can only connect Google Drive and Slack; other apps are not supported
options.explanation: The article lists Google Drive, Slack, Microsoft Teams, and implies more connectors are available.

options.text: AI connectors can be set up by any workspace member without admin approval
options.explanation: The guide advises working with your admin to set up AI Connectors, indicating admin involvement may be required.

questions.question: What advantage does Notion AI offer compared to using multiple standalone AI tools?

options.text: All AI capabilities are integrated into one workspace, reducing the need for multiple subscriptions
options.explanation: The article highlights that Notion AI consolidates functionality, saving on multiple tool costs.

options.text: It provides a free plan with unlimited AI usage
options.explanation: The Business trial gives full access, but it is not described as a free unlimited plan.

options.text: It only works for text generation, not for data analysis or database building
options.explanation: Notion AI also handles database building, data analysis, and more.

options.text: It requires separate installations for each AI model you want to use
options.explanation: Models are selectable within the same interface via the model picker.

questions.question: Which feature allows you to automatically generate formulas for a Notion database?

options.text: AI for databases – AI autofill can generate formulas based on a description
options.explanation: The guide mentions AI autofill can generate formulas when you describe what you need.

options.text: Conditional logic forms – they create formulas as part of the form workflow
options.explanation: Conditional logic forms adapt based on responses but do not generate database formulas.

options.text: One‑way database sync – it copies formulas from external tools like Jira
options.explanation: Syncs copy data, not generate new formulas.

options.text: Meeting notes AI – it extracts action items and turns them into formulas
options.explanation: Meeting notes produce summaries and action items, not database formulas.

questions.question: If you want the Personal Agent to reference the latest campaign updates from Slack when building a tracker, what must you do first?

options.text: Connect Slack as an AI connector and allow it to index the channel content
options.explanation: The article explains that connecting Slack lets the Agent pull recent updates for use in databases.

options.text: Export Slack messages to CSV and import them into Notion manually
options.explanation: Manual export is unnecessary once Slack is connected.

options.text: Create a separate Notion page for each Slack message
options.explanation: This would be inefficient and is not the recommended workflow.

options.text: Enable web search and let the Agent browse Slack’s public website
options.explanation: Slack is a private tool; you need to connect it directly, not rely on web search.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes how you can improve the accuracy of the Agent’s output when the initial result isn’t perfect?

options.text: Provide additional context or corrections and ask the Agent to adjust the result
options.explanation: The guide states that you can tell the Agent what to adjust, and it improves with more context.

options.text: Restart Notion and run the request again from scratch
options.explanation: Restarting isn’t required; refinement through feedback is the recommended method.

options.text: Switch to a different AI model and repeat the same prompt
options.explanation: While you can change models, the primary way to improve a specific result is to give more context.

options.text: Disable the Agent and use a third‑party AI tool instead
options.explanation: The guide encourages iterating with the Agent rather than abandoning it.

questions.question: What is a recommended approach for gradually building a workflow with Notion AI during the Business trial?

options.text: Start with a small, single task, then add one additional step once you’re comfortable
options.explanation: The article advises starting small, building from there, and adding steps progressively.

options.text: Create a massive multi‑step automation on day one to test all features at once
options.explanation: The guide recommends a gradual approach, not an overwhelming one‑shot setup.

options.text: Only use AI Meeting Notes and ignore other features until the trial ends
options.explanation: While Meeting Notes are highlighted, the guide encourages exploring many features.

options.text: Disable AI connectors until you have completed all manual workflows
options.explanation: Connecting tools enhances the Agent’s power; disabling them defeats the purpose.
